resume.name: Ryan Loo Tuck Fatt
resume.title: Full Stack Developer & AI Enthusiast
resume.profile: Software engineer with a CS background and a strong interest in building systems leveraging AI and LLM technologies. Built and shipped a modular AI agent platform with persistent memory, automated pipelines, sandboxed execution, and real-time integration — end to end, independently. Comfortable across the full SD stack but most at home on the AI layer.

featuredProjects.title: Clawcer Machine — AI Agent Assistant
featuredProjects.description: Developed a modular personal AI assistant platform with persistent memory, secure execution controls, and semi-automated workflows, integrated with Telegram for real-time interaction and task automation.
highlights: Engineered a privacy-focused web search and structured local storage system with RAG-like retrieval capabilities, enabling real-time data access and contextual continuity across sessions
Built an end-to-end automated resume generation pipeline — job parsing, relevance scoring, PDF generation, and delivery — reducing manual tailoring time by 90%
Developed a secure execution layer with command allowlisting, user approval prompts, and sandboxed wrapper scripts to mitigate unsafe operations, enforcing clear execution boundaries
Optimized system performance for low-resource environments through efficient process management, containerization, and volume-based data persistence
Established a scalable architecture for adding new AI skills and integrations, designed for ease of expansion
Contributed to full-stack development, focusing on front-end frameworks and PostgreSQL troubleshooting for scalable, efficient systems
Improved application performance through optimization of front-end code and database queries
Collaborated in a 17-person team, developing .NET web applications tailored to meet evolving business requirements
Modernized legacy web applications, migrating from IE to Chromium-based browsers improving compatibility and performance

achievements: President's List (2 semesters), Dean's List (4 semesters)
